The classification of Erythrodes stenocentron reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the gentian family.

intro: Erythrodes stenocentron, commonly known as the "green orchid," is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Erythrodes stenocentron include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
